reforms.description: The Irish government has implemented measures to support research and innovation, aiming to enhance the country's innovation system and foster entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research and innovation is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth through technological advancements.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively supported research initiatives as part of its broader economic strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the innovation landscape in Ireland, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government has implemented measures to support research and innovation, aiming to enhance the country's innovation system and foster entrepreneurship.

reforms.description: The Irish government has decided to focus a large part of its research spending on 14 priority areas to enhance innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Focusing research spending on priority areas is expected to enhance productivity and economic growth through targeted innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has made a decision to allocate research spending accordingly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the allocation of research funding, targeting areas with the highest potential for innovation and economic impact.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has expanded the innovation system from a relatively low base during the past two decades... it has decided to focus a large part of its research spending on 14 priority areas.

reforms.description: The government launched a review of the R&D tax credit to evaluate its effectiveness and consider adjustments to enhance its impact on innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reviewing and potentially enhancing the R&D tax credit is expected to incentivize private sector investment in innovation, leading to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched a review of the R&D tax credit.
reforms.importance_rationale: The review of the R&D tax credit is a significant step towards optimizing innovation incentives, although it does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: It is welcome that the government launched a review of the R&D tax credit, following an increase in its generosity over time.

reforms.description: The Government has introduced a credit guarantee scheme to alleviate funding challenges for innovation, particularly for small firms facing difficulties in accessing bank credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing guarantees, the scheme reduces the risk for lenders, thereby facilitating access to finance for innovative firms, which can enhance their growth and contribution to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The Government has recently introduced a credit guarantee scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the credit guarantee scheme represents a significant step in addressing funding challenges for innovative firms, enhancing their ability to access necessary capital.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has recently introduced a credit guarantee scheme.

reforms.description: Ireland's Science Foundation launched a new Research Centres programme to consolidate support for research into fewer centres of excellence, enhancing industry participation and contribution.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Consolidating research efforts into fewer centres is expected to enhance collaboration with industry, leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme was launched in 2012 and is set to establish 7 large-scale centres by 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how research is funded and organized in Ireland, with a substantial budget and industry involvement.
reforms.source_quote: SFI launched a new Research Centres programme to consolidate its supports into a smaller number of larger centres with enhanced industry contribution.

reforms.description: Ireland is assessing the effectiveness of its R&D tax credit scheme to ensure it focuses on additional R&D activity and reduces deadweight costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By refining the R&D tax credit scheme, the government aims to incentivize more genuine R&D activities, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A value-for-money review of the R&D tax credit is underway and expected to be completed by year-end.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the effectiveness of an existing instrument that supports innovation, but it does not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Further assess the effectiveness of the R&D tax credit scheme, and make it more focused on additional R&D activity.

reforms.description: Ireland expanded its public research funding and established Science Foundation Ireland (SFI) to promote research excellence in key sectors such as ICT and biotechnology, aiming to strengthen the country's innovation capabilities.
reforms.package_name: Strategy for Science, Technology and Innovation 2006-2013
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research enhances innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government expanded research funds and established SFI.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Ireland's approach to public research funding, aiming to enhance the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government expanded research funds available to the higher-education institutions and established a new agency, Science Foundation Ireland (SFI), to promote research excellence in ICT, biotechnology and, later on, energy.

reforms.description: An R&D tax credit was introduced in 2004 to incentivize private sector investment in research and development, aiming to boost innovation among firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit encourages firms to invest in R&D, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: An R&D tax credit was introduced in 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the R&D tax credit is a significant policy change aimed at enhancing private sector innovation, although it builds on existing frameworks.
reforms.source_quote: An R&D tax credit was introduced in 2004.

reforms.description: The government has introduced a hybrid R&D tax credit scheme that combines volume and incremental credits to incentivize R&D spending, particularly benefiting SMEs and encouraging higher growth in R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The scheme is designed to stimulate R&D investment by reducing costs for firms, particularly benefiting those with lower tax liabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has increased the generosity of the R&D tax credit scheme over time.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to R&D funding, enhancing the incentives for firms to invest in innovation and thereby potentially increasing overall economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2012 the government also introduced a hybrid of volume and incremental credit schemes.

reforms.description: Enterprise Ireland is establishing a Central Technology Transfer Office to enhance collaboration between higher education institutions and businesses, aiming to streamline technology transfer processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving technology transfer, the initiative is expected to facilitate innovation and commercialization of research outputs, benefiting both the economy and society.
reforms.status_evidence: Enterprise Ireland is also setting up a Central Technology Transfer Office.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to enhance the efficiency of technology transfer, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Enterprise Ireland is also setting up a Central Technology Transfer Office to be an advisory body for the 10 HEI-based TTOs.

reforms.description: The government should establish a pilot Research Technology Organisation (RTO) to provide technological solutions for firms, particularly SMEs, and enhance their innovation activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The RTO aims to bridge the gap between higher education institutions and the private sector, facilitating knowledge transfer and enhancing innovation capacity in SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should move to setting up a pilot RTO.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of an RTO represents a significant step towards enhancing the innovation ecosystem by providing targeted support for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The government should move to setting up a pilot RTO. The aim should be that the RTOs are eventually seen as 'the place to go' for technological solutions for firms, especially SMEs.

reforms.description: The government plans to enhance public procurement processes to better support SMEs and encourage innovation through the Procuring Innovation Initiative.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving procurement processes, the initiative aims to stimulate innovation in SMEs, leading to increased competition and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken steps in this direction with the new Procuring Innovation Initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to leverage public procurement as a tool for innovation, particularly benefiting SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The government has taken steps in this direction with the new Procuring Innovation Initiative to increase procurement of innovative solutions from SMEs.

reforms.description: The government is committed to studying the feasibility of a Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) programme to provide grants for R&D aligned with agency goals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The SBIR programme aims to enhance R&D funding for SMEs, fostering innovation and competitiveness in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is committed under the Jobs Action Plan 2013 to studying the feasibility of a SBIR.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed SBIR programme represents a significant potential enhancement to existing funding mechanisms for SMEs, promoting innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government is committed under the Jobs Action Plan 2013 to studying the feasibility of a SBIR.

reforms.description: The government has developed a new IPR protocol to improve the intellectual property rights regime, aiming to facilitate collaboration between firms and higher education institutions by addressing barriers in negotiating agreements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining IPR negotiations, the reform is expected to enhance collaboration between academia and industry, leading to increased innovation and commercialization of research.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has developed a new IPR protocol to improve the regime.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant barriers to collaboration in innovation, which is crucial for enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem in Ireland.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has developed a new IPR protocol to improve intellectual property rights agreements.

reforms.description: The government aims to improve broadband Internet infrastructure through the National Broadband Plan, which seeks to increase broadband speeds and accessibility across the country to foster innovation and enterprise growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband infrastructure is expected to enhance productivity and innovation across various sectors by providing better access to digital technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government’s National Broadband Plan aims to increase broadband speeds across the country.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant investment in national infrastructure that is critical for enabling innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s National Broadband Plan aims to increase broadband speeds across the country.

reforms.description: The Irish government aims to boost innovation in indigenous firms by enhancing their capacity to innovate and fostering stronger linkages with the higher education sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the innovation capacity of SMEs, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and create new growth opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to support innovation in indigenous firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the innovation ecosystem for domestic firms, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Raising SME capacity to innovate and to build greater linkages between enterprises and the higher education sector would provide a new engine of growth.

reforms.description: The government aims to simplify access to innovation support for firms by consolidating innovation funding and actions into fewer government agencies, enhancing the effectiveness and cost-efficiency of innovation and research policies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining access to innovation funding, the reform is expected to enhance the capacity of firms to innovate, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to consolidate innovation funding and actions into fewer agencies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how innovation support is delivered, which could have widespread implications for the effectiveness of innovation policy in Ireland.
reforms.source_quote: To increase the effectiveness and cost-efficiency of the innovation and research policies, and make it easier for businesses to access support, consolidate innovation funding and actions into a smaller number of Government agencies.

reforms.description: The government plans to independently and regularly evaluate all innovation policy tools to strengthen programs with proven higher returns and wind down those that are ineffective.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Regular evaluation of innovation policies is expected to enhance their effectiveness, leading to better allocation of resources and improved outcomes for firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to evaluate all actions in the area of innovation policy regularly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform signifies a major shift towards evidence-based policy-making in innovation, which could lead to more effective use of public resources.
reforms.source_quote: Reflecting significant uncertainties about the effectiveness of various innovation policy tools, independently and regularly evaluate all actions in this area, strengthen programmes with proven higher returns, and wind down the others.

reforms.description: The Momentum programme was launched to provide targeted training for the long-term unemployed in expanding employment areas such as ICT, digital media, and healthcare, aiming to enhance employability and address skill shortages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By equipping the long-term unemployed with skills relevant to growing sectors, the programme aims to improve their employability and contribute to economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is operational and providing training opportunities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme addresses specific skill shortages in the labor market and targets a vulnerable population, making it a significant but not structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: The recently launched Momentum programme seems to incorporate many of these elements.

reforms.description: The Springboard initiative was launched to offer part-time higher education courses for the unemployed, linking education directly to qualifications in growing sectors such as ICT and biopharma.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted education that aligns with labor market needs, the initiative aims to enhance the employability of participants and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is actively providing courses to unemployed individuals.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to align education with labor market needs, though it does not constitute a systemic overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Higher Education Authority has launched Springboard and ICT skills conversion programmes.

reforms.description: The Irish government plans to introduce JobPlus, a new scheme providing cash payments to employers who hire long-term unemployed individuals, aiming to improve youth employment outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing employers to hire long-term unemployed individuals, the scheme is expected to enhance job creation and reduce unemployment rates among youth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to introduce JobPlus to provide cash payments to employers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of JobPlus represents a significant policy shift aimed at addressing youth unemployment through financial incentives for employers.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to introduce JobPlus to provide cash payments to employers who hire unemployed workers.

reforms.description: To support youth entrepreneurship, the Irish government aims to better integrate entrepreneurship training into the educational system, starting from primary education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering entrepreneurial skills from a young age, the initiative is expected to enhance the self-employment rate among youth, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to better integrate entrepreneurship training into the educational system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance youth entrepreneurship through education, which could have lasting impacts on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: To develop entrepreneurship skills among youth, entrepreneurship and the option to work for oneself could be better integrated into the Irish educational system.

reforms.description: The government is shifting its policy focus to achieve greater economic returns from investments in innovation by targeting publicly-funded research in higher education institutions and public research organizations towards the needs of the enterprise sector, focusing on 14 priority areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning research with enterprise needs, the reform aims to enhance innovation capacity and economic returns, fostering growth and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is moving to target publicly-funded research as outlined in the Report of the Research Prioritisation Steering Group.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to public research funding, aiming to enhance the relevance and impact of research on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s policy focus has shifted to getting greater economic return from its investments in innovation.

reforms.description: The two Irish research councils have been merged into one, and Forfás will be integrated into the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation, streamlining the governance of innovation support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining governance structures can reduce overhead and improve the efficiency of innovation support, potentially enhancing the effectiveness of public funding.
reforms.status_evidence: The two Irish research councils have been combined into one.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in the governance of research funding, aimed at improving coordination and efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: The two Irish research councils (humanities and science) have been combined into one.

reforms.description: The government plans to create a national health innovation hub in 2014 to facilitate access for enterprises to the public hospital system for clinical drug trials, enhancing collaboration between the health and pharmaceutical sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving linkages between the health sector and enterprises, the initiative is expected to foster innovation and enhance the development of new medical solutions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to create a national health innovation hub in 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a health innovation hub represents a significant step towards enhancing collaboration in health research, which is crucial for innovation in the pharmaceutical sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government should also, as intended as part of its third level reform agenda, continue to move towards complementing core funding with a more comprehensive performance funding component than at present.

reforms.description: The government aims to enhance the framework conditions for entrepreneurship and innovation by improving access to capital, intellectual property regimes, and broadband Internet, which are essential for fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By creating a more conducive environment for startups, the initiative is expected to stimulate innovation and job creation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to enhance the framework conditions for entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses multiple barriers to entrepreneurship, which is vital for economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Beyond innovation specific policies it is important to establish a business environment where entrepreneurship and innovation can flourish.
